title: Flaky Roasted Peruvian Potatoes Causa Limena
description: A solo dinner Peruvian dish featuring roasted potatoes, amazake, and shio-koji-cure, presented in a causa limena form in a ramekin vessel. This flexitarian-friendly dish is a twist on traditional Peruvian cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the cubed potatoes with olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
While the potatoes are roasting, mix the amazake, shio-koji, garlic, and lime juice in a small bowl.
Once the potatoes are done, let them cool for a few minutes before stirring in the amazake mixture.
Spoon the potato mixture into a ramekin vessel, smoothing the top with a spoon.
Refrigerate the causa limena for at least 30 minutes to allow it to set.
Just before serving, garnish with chopped fresh cilantro.
Serve the flaky roasted Peruvian potatoes causa limena chilled, in the ramekin vessel.
